text:
Good news-the Query Sentinel logic is actually pretty straightforward. Here's the breakdown:
Core Logic
The policy kills long-running queries when the system is overloaded.
Overload Detection (lines 294-345 in lib.rs)
The system is considered "overloaded" when either condition is true (depending on config flags):
1. Memory overload (use_memory_overload):
(cached_memory + free_memory) / total_memory < overload_available_memory_ratio
1. Default threshold: 5% available memory
2. CPU overload (use_cpu_overload) - requires both:
- CPU pressure rate ≥ overload_cpu_pressure_rate (default: 99%)
- AND CPU usertsystem time ≥ overload_cpu_user_system_time_rate (default: 95%)
- AND CPU idle time « overload_cpu_idle_time_rate (default: 1%)
Tuning Trigger
Overload must persist for N consecutive ticks before action is taken:
overload
_ticks_counter ›= overload_ticks_tuning
(default: 1 tick)
If overload clears, the counter resets to 0.
Kill Logic (lines 348-401)
When tuning activates:
1. Run $currentop with configurable filters
2. Sort by secs_running descending (longest first), then numyields ascending
3. Kill up to max_operations_to_kill (default: 10) operations per tick
Default Filters
- Only user connections (excludes mongotune itself)
- Only operations running ≥ 1 second (microsecs_running >= 1_000_000)
---—
The formulas are simple ratios and thresholds-no complex math. The main subtlety is that CPU overload
to be high, which prevents false positives.
